# UC-22.14.1 · Centralized Audit Event Logging Policy Coverage

## Description

Shows which systems and sourcetypes are forwarding security-relevant events so auditors can evidence that logging policy scope matches AU-2 event types.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes os, sec. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Maintain `nist_au2_event_coverage.csv` mapping sourcetypes to AU-2 event families; (2) schedule daily and alert on sourcetypes with `required=true` and zero volume; (3) onboard missing forwarders or enable missing channels; (4) attach evidence export for assessors. Indexes required: `index=os`, `index=sec`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=sec` OR `index=os` — Windows Security, Linux auditd, cloud control planes (sourcetypes vary by TA).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain `nist_au2_event_coverage.csv` mapping sourcetypes to AU-2 event families; (2) schedule daily and alert on sourcetypes with `required=true` and zero volume; (3) onboard missing forwarders or enable missing channels; (4) attach evidence export for assessors.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index IN (sec, os, cloud) earliest=-24h
| stats dc(host) as hosts, dc(sourcetype) as sourcetypes, count as events by index, sourcetype
| lookup nist_au2_event_coverage.csv sourcetype OUTPUT au2_event_family, required
| eval gap=if(required="true" AND events=0, "No_Data", "OK")
| sort index, sourcetype
```

Understanding this SPL

**Centralized Audit Event Logging Policy Coverage (AU-2)** — Shows which systems and sourcetypes are forwarding security-relevant events so auditors can evidence that logging policy scope matches AU-2 event types.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=sec` OR `index=os` — Windows Security, Linux auditd, cloud control planes (sourcetypes vary by TA). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by index, sourcetype** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **gap** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (coverage by sourcetype), Single value (percent required sources reporting), Bar chart (event volume by family)

## SPL

```spl
index IN (sec, os, cloud) earliest=-24h
| stats dc(host) as hosts, dc(sourcetype) as sourcetypes, count as events by index, sourcetype
| lookup nist_au2_event_coverage.csv sourcetype OUTPUT au2_event_family, required
| eval gap=if(required="true" AND events=0, "No_Data", "OK")
| sort index, sourcetype
```

## Visualization

Table (coverage by sourcetype), Single value (percent required sources reporting), Bar chart (event volume by family)
